headline: the long way home, alone this time
mood_description: Quiet at first, like talking to yourself in a parked car, until the feeling gets too big for the room and the whole thing floods.
interpretation: A milestone that was supposed to be shared, reached alone. The license was a promise made to someone who is already gone, so passing the test only measures the distance between them. It is the moment a private heartbreak turns into an ordinary errand she now has to drive through.

themes.name: The promise that outlived the couple
themes.description: She passes the test she swore she would pass for him, and the win only counts the miles he is not in the car for.

themes.name: Suburban geography of grief
themes.description: Streets, stop signs, his neighborhood. The map of a small town doubles as a map of where it hurts.

journey.description: Just her voice and a few chords, a diary left open on the dashboard.
Red lights and stop signs blur. The harmonies stack up like thoughts she cannot stop having.
The dam breaks. What started as a whisper ends as the loudest she has been all song.

key_lines.line: I got my driver's license like I told you I would
key_lines.insight: A win that only counts as a loss. The accomplishment is addressed to someone who is not there to hear it.

key_lines.line: And you're probably with that blonde girl
key_lines.insight: Heartbreak does its worst work in the imagination, casting the exact scene it dreads.

sonic_texture: A ballad that grows a spine. Sparse piano and a small voice swell into stacked harmonies and a pounding bridge, mirroring a feeling that refuses to stay small.
